ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0EJM (Carbonated soft drinks) is the closest defensible category; no brand-specific code exists for Bei Bingyang.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for this branded carbonated beverage; left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard bottle or can of Bei Bingyang carbonated soda is approximately 330 g, providing about 130 kcal and 33 g of sugar per serving.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Beverages, carbonated, cola, with higher fructose corn syrup and fruit flavors (SR Legacy); cross-referenced with USDA SR Legacy entry 14144 for fruit-flavored carbonated soft drink with sugar
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Bei Bingyang is a branded Chinese carbonated fruit soda not individually listed in USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for generic fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drink with sugar (FDC ID 170369), which reports approximately 40 kcal/100 g, ~10 g total carbohydrate/100 g as sugar, negligible protein and fat, and ~3 mg sodium/100 g. These values align closely with the input record (energy 39.39 kcal, carbohydrate 10 g, sugar 10 g, sodium 3.03 mg, protein 0 g, fat 0 g). Input values are consistent with this approved USDA reference and are retained. GrabFood was the original data source in the input record but is a forbidden primary source; values are accepted only because they are independently corroborated by the USDA SR Legacy approved reference.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: Bei Bingyang is a sugar-sweetened carbonated fruit soda with 10 g sugar/100 g. No direct GI measurement exists for this brand in any approved source. GI of 63 is taken from the University of Sydney GI Database and the 2008 International Tables (Atkinson et al., Diabetes Care 2008;31(12):2281-2283) for generic carbonated soft drink / sugar-sweetened soda, which is the closest defensible analogous food from an approved GI source. Glycemic load per 330 g serving = (63 × 33 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 20.8.

hypertension.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage with 10 g sugar/100 g (33 g per standard 330 g serving); AHA 2018 identifies SSBs as independently raising blood pressure risk via insulin-mediated sodium retention and weight gain, irrespective of the beverage's low sodium content (3 mg/100 g).
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid Bei Bingyang and all sugar-sweetened carbonated beverages; substitute with plain water, unsweetened sparkling water, or low-sugar alternatives to reduce SSB-driven blood pressure elevation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar-sweetened beverage delivering 33 g of rapidly absorbed free sugar per 330 g serving with zero dietary fibre; liquid sugar bypasses satiety mechanisms and causes acute postprandial blood glucose spikes, with GI 63 and glycemic load 20.8 per serving.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes must avoid Bei Bingyang; the high free-sugar content and medium-to-high glycemic load per serving cause rapid blood glucose elevation that cannot be mitigated by portion reduction to a clinically safe level.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Regular consumption of sugar-sweetened beverages is independently associated with increased cardiovascular risk through elevated triglycerides, insulin resistance, visceral adiposity, and systemic inflammation; each 330 g serving delivers 33 g free sugar with no protective nutrients.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should eliminate sugar-sweetened carbonated beverages such as Bei Bingyang from their diet and replace them with plain water or unsweetened beverages to reduce cardiometabolic risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High free-sugar content (10 g/100 g) with zero anti-inflammatory nutrients; refined sugar promotes pro-inflammatory cytokine activity relevant to RA disease activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid sugar-sweetened beverages such as Bei Bingyang and choose anti-inflammatory alternatives such as green tea or plain water to reduce systemic inflammatory burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Bei Bingyang (北冰洋汽水, literally 'North Arctic Ocean soda') is an iconic Chinese carbonated fruit soda originally produced in Beijing, China, dating back to the 1950s. It is one of the most recognisable retro or vintage Chinese soft drinks, widely associated with nostalgia in mainland China and popular among Chinese communities in Singapore and Southeast Asia. The beverage is a clear to lightly coloured, sweet, fruit-flavoured carbonated drink — most commonly orange-flavoured — with a refreshing, effervescent character and a straightforward sweet taste profile. It is commercially manufactured, ultra-processed, and sold in glass bottles or cans, typically served chilled. Nutritionally, Bei Bingyang is a sugar-sweetened beverage (SSB) providing approximately 39 kcal per 100 g, derived almost entirely from 10 g of free sugar per 100 g. It contains negligible protein (0 g), negligible fat (0 g), and negligible dietary fibre (0 g), with a very low sodium content of approximately 3 mg per 100 g. A standard 330 g serving delivers approximately 130 kcal and 33 g of free sugar. Clinically, Bei Bingyang is classified as an SSB and is unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. Its glycemic index is estimated at 63 (medium) based on analogous carbonated soft drink data from the University of Sydney GI Database, with a glycemic load of approximately 20.8 per serving — a clinically significant load. The beverage provides no protective nutrients, no fibre, and no micronutrients of note. It is vegetarian, vegan, and gluten-free by formulation.

confidence_reason: Bei Bingyang is not individually listed in USDA FoodData Central, Singapore HPB Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or FAO/INFOODS SEA databases. Nutrient values are adapted from the closest approved-source analogue (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy generic fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drink with sugar, FDC ID 170369), which aligns closely with the input values. Confidence is medium because the values are consistent across multiple reference points but are not sourced from a direct brand-specific approved-source entry. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for generic carbonated soft drink (Atkinson et al. 2008).
notes: 1. ZERO-MACRO JUSTIFICATION: protein_g is naturally zero — Bei Bingyang is a sugar-water carbonated beverage containing no protein-bearing ingredients by formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy analogous carbonated soft drink reference (FDC ID 170369). fat_g is naturally zero — the product contains no fat-bearing ingredients by formulation; confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy reference. saturated_fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation. cholesterol_mg is confirmed zero by product formulation. dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this is a clear carbonated sugar-water beverage with no plant fibre components. trans_fat_g is confirmed zero by product formulation. 2. NEGATIVE SENTINEL VALUES (-1): monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1 — detailed fatty acid profile data for this branded carbonated beverage is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2026. potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1 — micronutrient and moisture data for this branded Chinese carbonated soda are not individually reported in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Foodo as of 2026; the generic carbonated soft drink reference does not provide reliable micronutrient values for this specific product. 3. SOURCE ADAPTATION: Input values from GrabFood (energy 39.39 kcal, carbohydrate 10 g, sugar 10 g, sodium 3.03 mg, protein 0 g, fat 0 g) are consistent with USDA SR Legacy data for generic fruit-flavoured carbonated soft drink (FDC ID 170369) and have been retained. GrabFood is a forbidden primary source; values are accepted only because they are independently corroborated by the approved USDA FoodData Central reference. The nutrient_source field cites only the approved USDA source. 4. ONTOLOGY: No exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 code exists for Bei Bingyang; broader carbonated beverage codes used with gap notes. FoodOn FOODON_03301589 and FoodEx2 A0EJM are the closest defensible anchors. 5. ETHNIC TAG CORRECTION: Input ethnic_tags included 'Noodles' which is clearly erroneous for a beverage; this tag has been removed. 6. GI ESTIMATION: GI of 63 estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous carbonated soft drink entry (Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283); no direct GI measurement for Bei Bingyang brand exists in approved sources. GL per 330 g serving = (63 × 33) / 100 = 20.8.

veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Bei Bingyang Carbonated Soda passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8. The primary fix in iteration 3 was ensuring g2 compliance by citing only USDA FoodData Central as the approved nutrient source (removing the dual-organization citation that included HPB Singapore as a co-primary source) and ensuring the GI source explicitly names the University of Sydney GI Database with the full Atkinson et al. 2008 peer-reviewed journal reference. All zero macros are justified with 'naturally zero' and 'confirmed zero' language. All -1 sentinel values are documented. Ontology anchors are defensible with gap notes. Multilingual naming satisfies the Chinese cuisine requirement. Aliases are consistent between flat array and aliases_by_language.
